message:
chore(ntm): untrack per-machine runtime state; preserve session summaries

Untracks the five NTM runtime-state files that were baked into the
repo at some earlier point and have been showing up as modified on
every session since — they change on every agent tick and have no
value in history:

  .ntm/logs/am-mcp_agent_mail_rust.log
  .ntm/logs/cm-mcp_agent_mail_rust.log
  .ntm/pids/am-mcp_agent_mail_rust.pid
  .ntm/pids/cm-mcp_agent_mail_rust.pid
  .ntm/rate_limits.json

Uses `git rm --cached` — the files stay on disk, they're just no
longer tracked by git. Subsequent agent runs will regenerate them
locally without polluting `git status`.

`.gitignore` is narrowed from the original catch-all `.ntm/` (which
would also have hidden the three tracked `.ntm/summaries/*.json`
session-handoff records) to the specific runtime-state patterns:

  .ntm/logs/
  .ntm/pids/
  .ntm/rate_limits.json
  .ntm/*.lock

The historical session summaries under `.ntm/summaries/` stay
tracked — they are the NTM handoff artifacts agents write at the
end of a session (they contain accomplishments + file action
history + context) and are explicitly the kind of thing we *do*
want in history. The user's global `~/.gitignore` still has the
broader `.ntm/` rule, which applies to all other repos on this
machine; the repo-level narrowing here is the targeted exception
for the tracked summaries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -300,8 +300,13 @@ test_*
 !test_*.rs
 !test_*.sh
 
-# NTM (agent tmux orchestrator) runtime state — per-machine, never tracked
-.ntm/
+# NTM (agent tmux orchestrator) runtime state — per-machine churn, never tracked.
+# (Session summaries under .ntm/summaries/ are historical handoff records
+# and remain tracked deliberately.)
+.ntm/logs/
+.ntm/pids/
+.ntm/rate_limits.json
+.ntm/*.lock
 
 # ATC rollup scratch test databases (SQLite, created under crates/*)
 crates/**/atc_rollup_test_*
